description_long: This object is a large, vintage-style box camera constructed primarily from wood, featuring a rectangular housing with a sliding bellows mechanism extending from one side. The main body has a flat top with a recessed rectangular opening, likely for viewing or loading photographic plates, and is reinforced with metal corner brackets. On one side, a circular lens opening is visible, surrounded by a wooden panel, while the opposite side reveals a brass component, possibly part of the shutter or internal mechanism. The camera is mounted on a sturdy wooden base supported by metal rods, providing stability and elevating the device. The bellows section is made of a dark, accordion-like material, allowing for adjustable focal length, and the rear of the camera has a circular port for viewing or film loading. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century field or studio cameras, intended for stationary use.
description: A large, vintage wooden box camera with a sliding bellows, brass fittings, and a sturdy base, featuring a circular lens opening and a recessed top panel.
description_view_0: Side view showing the extended bellows, circular lens opening, and the main wooden housing with a flat top.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the brass internal component and the camera's rectangular structure.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the bellows retracted and the main housing visible, mirroring view 0.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the end of the bellows and a circular port, emphasizing the camera's depth and construction.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
